response:
jamul indian village is known for its rich cultural heritage and strong sense of community the village is planning to build a new community center with a unique design the center will be constructed in the shape of a regular hexagon with each side measuring 30 meters the community also decided to incorporate a circular garden in the center of the hexagon with the garden's circumference touching the midpoints of the hexagon's sides  

the area of the hexagonal community center can be calculated by dividing it into six equilateral triangles each with side length 30 meters the area of one equilateral triangle is (sqrt(3)/4) * (30)^2 the total area of the hexagon is six times this value the radius of the circumscribed circle of the hexagon is 30 meters the area of the circular garden can be calculated using the formula for the area of a circle with radius r the radius of the garden is 15 meters the area of the garden is pi * (15)^2  

is there anything else i can help with?
